notes.succeeded_noem: Became 34th Governor in January 2025 when Kristi Noem became DHS Secretary
notes.pro_life: Spoke out against abortion during 2014 Senate campaign
notes.guns: Backed bills to arm volunteers in schools
notes.limited_government: Called himself 'conservative voice for limited government'
notes.acu: American Conservative Union highest ranking in 2017 as state legislator

profile.religion: Christian (serves as Deacon of his home church, grew up in the church, graduated Sunshine Bible Academy)
profile.education: Sunshine Bible Academy (1977), South Dakota National Guard (1978-1985)
profile.background: Rancher; South Dakota state representative (2001-2009) and state senator (2009-2015, 2017-2019); lieutenant governor (2019-2025); became 34th governor in 2025 after Kristi Noem resigned.

notes.senate_majority_leader: Elected Senate Majority Leader in November 2024, replacing Mitch McConnell. Took office January 3, 2025
notes.christian_faith: Attended evangelical Biola University, described faith as keeping him 'grounded' in his career
notes.fiscal: Generally conservative fiscal record with pragmatic streak as leader
notes.agriculture: Strong advocate for South Dakota agriculture
notes.leadership: No. 1 Senate Republican, serves as chamber's presiding leader

profile.religion: Evangelical Protestant (attended Biola University, evangelical Christian institution)
profile.education: Biola University (BA Business 1983), University of South Dakota (MBA)
profile.background: Senate Majority Leader since 2025. Former U.S. House member and state railroad director. Former executive director of South Dakota Republican Party. In Senate since 2005.

notes.youth: Introduced Developing and Empowering our Aspiring Leaders Act
notes.moderate_streak: Heritage Action scores vary widely (33% to 88%), indicating pragmatic voting

profile.religion: Catholic (lifelong Catholic, Knights of Columbus member)
profile.education: South Dakota State University (BS Political Science 1977)
profile.background: Former Governor of South Dakota and insurance/real estate executive. Former South Dakota State Senator. In Senate since 2015.

notes.moderate_republican: Member of Problem Solvers Caucus, considered moderate Republican
notes.governor_run: Announced June 2025 he will run for South Dakota governor in 2026
notes.sunday_school: Active Sunday School teacher and community volunteer

profile.religion: Christian (Sunday School teacher, active church volunteer)
profile.education: University of South Dakota (BA Political Science 1999, Omicron Delta Kappa honors), University of Kansas (MPA 2002), Truman Scholar (1998)
